mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE < 3.5% periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no correlation with INDPRO-HOUST growth or Rank IC < 0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 300 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL > 300 or FEDFUNDS rising without negative impact.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) signal robust demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through increased corporate revenue.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no correlation with RSAFS-GDPC1 trends or Rank IC < 0.03 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through consumer spending growth.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative despite PAYEMS > 150k or Rank IC < 0.02 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) indicate sustained demand momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through infrastructure investment.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no link to HOUST-GDPC1 trends or Rank IC < 0.04 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103 signals improved manufacturing capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative despite INDPRO > 103 or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.
